12	// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
13	// limitations under the License.
14	//
15	// Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
16	//
17	// RBTDRF — fast-tier test cases for theurge
18	//
19	// Ports enrollment-validation (47), regime-validation (21), and regime-smoke (7)
20	// from bash test framework to theurge. Cases shell out to bash — theurge invokes
21	// the actual bash utilities and asserts on exit codes. No reimplementation.
22	
23	use std::path::Path;
24	use std::process::Command;
25	
26	use crate::case;
27	use crate::rbtdre_engine::{rbtdre_Case, rbtdre_Disposition, rbtdre_Fixture, rbtdre_Verdict};
28	use crate::rbtdri_invocation::rbtdri_find_tabtarget_global;
29	use crate::rbtdgc_consts::{
30	    RBTDGC_HYGIENE_CHECK_DOCKERFILE,
31	    RBTDGC_HYGIENE_CHECK_VESSEL,
32	    RBTDGC_LIST_DEPOT,
33	    RBTDGC_RBRS_FILE,
34	    RBTDGC_RENDER_NAMEPLATE,
35	    RBTDGC_RENDER_PAYOR,
36	    RBTDGC_RENDER_REPO,
37	    RBTDGC_RENDER_STATION,
38	    RBTDGC_RENDER_VESSEL,
39	    RBTDGC_UNMAKE_DEPOT,
40	    RBTDGC_VALIDATE_NAMEPLATE,
41	    RBTDGC_VALIDATE_PAYOR,
42	    RBTDGC_VALIDATE_REPO,
43	    RBTDGC_VALIDATE_STATION,
44	    RBTDGC_VALIDATE_VESSEL,
45	};
46	use crate::rbtdrm_manifest::{
47	    RBTDRM_FIXTURE_DOCKERFILE_HYGIENE,
48	    RBTDRM_FIXTURE_ENROLLMENT_VALIDATION,
49	    RBTDRM_FIXTURE_REGIME_SMOKE,
50	    RBTDRM_FIXTURE_REGIME_VALIDATION,
51	    RBTDRM_MODULE_RBRD,
52	    RBTDRM_MODULE_RBRN,
53	    RBTDRM_MODULE_RBRR,
54	    RBTDRM_MODULE_RBRV,
55	    RBTDRM_PROBATE_RBRD,
56	    RBTDRM_PROBATE_RBRN,
57	    RBTDRM_PROBATE_RBRR,
58	    RBTDRM_PROBATE_RBRV,
59	};
60	use crate::rbtdrx_platform::rbtdrx_native_to_posix;
61	
62	// ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
63	
64	/// Sub-assertion within a case: run bash snippet, check exit code.
65	struct RbtdrfSub {
66	    label: &'static str,
67	    setup: &'static str,
68	    command: &'static str,
69	    expect_ok: bool,
70	}
71	
72	impl RbtdrfSub {
73	    const fn ok(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str) -> Self {
74	        Self { label, setup, command: "buv_vet \"TEST\"", expect_ok: true }
75	    }
76	    const fn fatal(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str) -> Self {
77	        Self { label, setup, command: "buv_vet \"TEST\"", expect_ok: false }
78	    }
79	    const fn ok_cmd(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str, command: &'static str) -> Self {
80	        Self { label, setup, command, expect_ok: true }
81	    }
82	    const fn fatal_cmd(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str, command: &'static str) -> Self {
83	        Self { label, setup, command, expect_ok: false }
84	    }
85	}
86	
87	/// Run a bash script, return (exit_code, stdout, stderr). Saves traces to case dir.
88	fn rbtdrf_run_bash(
89	    project_root: &Path,
90	    script: &str,
91	    dir: &Path,
92	    trace_prefix: &str,
93	) -> Result<(i32, String, String), String> {
94	    let output = Command::new("bash")
95	        .arg("-c")
96	        .arg(script)
97	        .current_dir(project_root)
98	        .output()
99	        .map_err(|e| format!("bash execution failed: {}", e))?;
100	
101	    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).into_owned();
102	    let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).into_owned();
103	    let code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);
104	
105	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-script.sh", trace_prefix)), script);
106	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stdout.txt", trace_prefix)), &stdout);
107	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stderr.txt", trace_prefix)), &stderr);
108	
109	    Ok((code, stdout, stderr))
110	}
111	
112	/// Run enrollment-validation sub-assertions against BUV.
113	fn rbtdrf_run_ev(
114	    dir: &Path,
115	    enrollment: &str,
116	    subs: &[RbtdrfSub],
117	) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
118	    let root = match std::env::current_dir() {
119	        Ok(r) => r,
120	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("cannot get cwd: {}", e)),
121	    };
122	    let buv = root.join("Tools/buk/buv_validation.sh");
123	
124	    for (i, sub) in subs.iter().enumerate() {
125	        let script = format!(
126	            "set -euo pipefail\nsource '{}'\nzbuv_kindle\nzbuv_reset_enrollment\n{}\n{}\n{}",
127	            rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&buv),
128	            enrollment,
129	            sub.setup,
130	            sub.command,
131	        );
132	
133	        match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script, dir, &format!("sub-{}", i)) {
134	            Ok((code, _, _)) => {
135	                let ok = code == 0;
136	                if ok != sub.expect_ok {
137	                    return if sub.expect_ok {
138	                        rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected ok, got exit {}", sub.label, code))
139	                    } else {
140	                        rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected failure, got exit 0", sub.label))
141	                    };
142	                }
143	            }
144	            Err(e) => {
145	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: {}", sub.label, e));
146	            }
147	        }
148	    }
149	    rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
150	}
151	
152	/// Drive a regime's public `*_probate` function against a synthetic
153	/// baseline+override written to the case temp dir. `expect_ok` selects the
154	/// verdict polarity: pass-anchors validate the pristine baseline (true);
155	/// negatives apply one violating override and expect non-zero (false).
156	/// Sources only the contract surface — `buv_validation.sh` plus the one regime
157	/// module — mirroring the `buv_vet` bridge that enrollment-validation uses: no
158	/// internal-module chain, no z-private reach.
159	fn rbtdrf_run_probate(
160	    dir: &Path,
161	    module: &str,
162	    probate_fn: &str,
163	    baseline: &str,
164	    override_: &str,
165	    expect_ok: bool,
166	    label: &str,
167	) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
168	    let root = match std::env::current_dir() {
169	        Ok(r) => r,
170	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("cannot get cwd: {}", e)),
171	    };
172	    let buv = root.join("Tools/buk/buv_validation.sh");
173	    let module_path = root.join("Tools/rbk").join(module);
174	
175	    let env_file = dir.join(format!("{}.env", label));
176	    let body = format!("{}\n{}\n", baseline, override_);
177	    if let Err(e) = std::fs::write(&env_file, &body) {
178	        return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: write env failed: {}", label, e));
179	    }
180	
181	    let script = format!(
182	        "set -euo pipefail\n\
183	         source '{}'\n\
184	         source '{}'\n\
185	         zbuv_kindle\n\
186	         {} '{}'",
187	        rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&buv),
188	        rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&module_path),
189	        probate_fn,
190	        rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&env_file),
191	    );
192	
193	    match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script, dir, label) {
194	        Ok((code, _, _)) => {
195	            let ok = code == 0;
196	            if ok == expect_ok {
197	                rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
198	            } else if expect_ok {
199	                rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected ok, got exit {}", label, code))
200	            } else {
201	                rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected failure, got exit 0", label))
202	            }
203	        }
204	        Err(e) => rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: {}", label, e)),
205	    }
206	}
207	
208	/// Run a tabtarget and check exit 0.
209	fn rbtdrf_run_tt(
210	    project_root: &Path,
211	    colophon: &str,
212	    args: &[&str],
213	    dir: &Path,
214	    label: &str,
215	) -> Result<(), String> {
216	    let tt = rbtdri_find_tabtarget_global(project_root, colophon)?;
217	    let output = Command::new(&tt)
218	        .args(args)
219	        .current_dir(project_root)
220	        .output()
221	        .map_err(|e| format!("{}: failed to run {}: {}", label, tt.display(), e))?;
222	
223	    let code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);
224	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stdout.txt", label)), &output.stdout);
225	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stderr.txt", label)), &output.stderr);
226	
227	    if code != 0 {
228	        return Err(format!(
229	            "{}: {} exited {} — {}",
230	            label,
231	            colophon,
232	            code,
233	            String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr),
234	        ));
235	    }
236	    Ok(())
237	}
238	
239	/// Run a tabtarget that is expected to fail. Inverts rbtdrf_run_tt:
240	/// non-zero exit returns Ok(()), zero exit returns Err. Invocation errors
241	/// (tabtarget not found, launcher failure) still propagate as Err.
242	fn rbtdrf_run_tt_neg(
243	    project_root: &Path,
244	    colophon: &str,
245	    args: &[&str],
246	    dir: &Path,
247	    label: &str,
248	) -> Result<(), String> {
249	    let tt = rbtdri_find_tabtarget_global(project_root, colophon)?;
250	    let output = Command::new(&tt)
251	        .args(args)
252	        .current_dir(project_root)
253	        .output()
254	        .map_err(|e| format!("{}: failed to run {}: {}", label, tt.display(), e))?;
255	
256	    let code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);
257	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stdout.txt", label)), &output.stdout);
258	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stderr.txt", label)), &output.stderr);
259	
260	    if code == 0 {
261	        return Err(format!(
262	            "{}: {} expected failure, got exit 0",
263	            label, colophon,
264	        ));
265	    }
266	    Ok(())
267	}
268	
269	// ── Enrollment-validation cases ─────────────────────────────
270	
271	// --- Length types ---
272	
273	fn rbtdrf_ev_string_valid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
274	    rbtdrf_run_ev(dir,
275	        "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Strings\"\n\
276	         buv_string_enroll TEST_NAME 1 20 \"Test name\"\n\
277	         buv_string_enroll TEST_DESC 3 50 \"Test description\"",
278	        &[RbtdrfSub::ok("valid strings",
279	            "export TEST_NAME=\"hello\"\nexport TEST_DESC=\"a valid description\"")],
280	    )
281	}
282	
283	fn rbtdrf_ev_string_empty_optional(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
284	    rbtdrf_run_ev(dir,
285	        "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Strings\"\n\
286	         buv_string_enroll TEST_OPT 0 20 \"Optional field\"",
287	        &[RbtdrfSub::ok("empty optional", "export TEST_OPT=\"\"")],
288	    )
289	}
290	
291	fn rbtdrf_ev_string_too_short(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
292	    rbtdrf_run_ev(dir,
293	        "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Strings\"\n\
294	         buv_string_enroll TEST_NAME 5 20 \"Test name\"",
295	        &[RbtdrfSub::fatal("too short", "export TEST_NAME=\"ab\"")],
296	    )
297	}
298	
299	fn rbtdrf_ev_string_too_long(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
300	    rbtdrf_run_ev(dir,
301	        "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Strings\"\n\
302	         buv_string_enroll TEST_NAME 1 5 \"Test name\"",
303	        &[RbtdrfSub::fatal("too long", "export TEST_NAME=\"toolongvalue\"")],
304	    )
305	}
306	
307	fn rbtdrf_ev_string_empty_required(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
308	    rbtdrf_run_ev(dir,
309	        "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Strings\"\n\
310	         buv_string_enroll TEST_NAME 1 20 \"Test name\"",
311	        &[RbtdrfSub::fatal("empty required", "export TEST_NAME=\"\"")],
312	    )
313	}
314	
315	fn rbtdrf_ev_xname_valid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
316	    rbtdrf_run_ev(dir,
317	        "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Xnames\"\n\
318	         buv_xname_enroll TEST_IDENT 2 12 \"Identifier\"",
319	        &[
320	            RbtdrfSub::ok("standard xname", "export TEST_IDENT=\"myName\""),
321	            RbtdrfSub::ok("underscore and hyphen", "export TEST_IDENT=\"my_var-1\""),
322	        ],
323	    )
324	}
325	
326	fn rbtdrf_ev_xname_invalid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
327	    rbtdrf_run_ev(dir,
328	        "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Xnames\"\n\
329	         buv_xname_enroll TEST_IDENT 2 12 \"Identifier\"",
330	        &[
331	            RbtdrfSub::fatal("starts with digit", "export TEST_IDENT=\"1bad\""),
332	            RbtdrfSub::fatal("contains dot", "export TEST_IDENT=\"my.name\""),
333	            RbtdrfSub::fatal("too short", "export TEST_IDENT=\"x\""),
334	            RbtdrfSub::fatal("too long", "export TEST_IDENT=\"abcdefghijklm\""),
335	        ],
336	    )
337	}
338	
339	fn rbtdrf_ev_gname_valid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
340	    rbtdrf_run_ev(dir,
341	        "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Gnames\"\n\
342	         buv_gname_enroll TEST_PROJECT 3 20 \"Project ID\"",
343	        &[RbtdrfSub::ok("valid gname", "export TEST_PROJECT=\"my-project-01\"")],
344	    )
345	}
346	
347	fn rbtdrf_ev_gname_invalid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
348	    rbtdrf_run_ev(dir,
349	        "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Gnames\"\n\
350	         buv_gname_enroll TEST_PROJECT 3 20 \"Project ID\"",
351	        &[
352	            RbtdrfSub::fatal("uppercase", "export TEST_PROJECT=\"MyProject\""),
353	            RbtdrfSub::fatal("ends with hyphen", "export TEST_PROJECT=\"my-project-\""),
354	            RbtdrfSub::fatal("starts with digit", "export TEST_PROJECT=\"1project\""),
355	        ],
356	    )
357	}
358	
359	fn rbtdrf_ev_fqin_valid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
360	    rbtdrf_run_ev(dir,
361	        "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"FQINs\"\n\
362	         buv_fqin_enroll TEST_IMAGE 5 100 \"Image reference\"",
363	        &[RbtdrfSub::ok("valid fqin",
364	            "export TEST_IMAGE=\"us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-proj/repo/image:latest\"")],
365	    )
366	}
367	
368	fn rbtdrf_ev_fqin_invalid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
369	    rbtdrf_run_ev(dir,
370	        "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"FQINs\"\n\
371	         buv_fqin_enroll TEST_IMAGE 5 100 \"Image reference\"",
372	        &[
373	            RbtdrfSub::fatal("special char", "export TEST_IMAGE=\".invalid/path\""),
374	            RbtdrfSub::fatal("empty", "export TEST_IMAGE=\"\""),
375	        ],
376	    )
377	}
378	
379	// --- Choice types ---
380	
381	fn rbtdrf_ev_bool_valid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
382	    rbtdrf_run_ev(dir,
383	        "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Booleans\"\n\
384	         buv_bool_enroll TEST_ENABLED \"Feature enabled\"",
385	        &[
386	            RbtdrfSub::ok("value 1", "export TEST_ENABLED=\"1\""),
387	            RbtdrfSub::ok("value 0", "export TEST_ENABLED=\"0\""),
388	        ],
389	    )
390	}
391	
392	fn rbtdrf_ev_bool_invalid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
393	    rbtdrf_run_ev(dir,
394	        "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Booleans\"\n\
395	         buv_bool_enroll TEST_ENABLED \"Feature enabled\"",
396	        &[
397	            RbtdrfSub::fatal("string true", "export TEST_ENABLED=\"true\""),
398	            RbtdrfSub::fatal("string yes", "export TEST_ENABLED=\"yes\""),
399	            RbtdrfSub::fatal("number 2", "export TEST_ENABLED=\"2\""),
400	        ],
401	    )
402	}
403	
404	fn rbtdrf_ev_bool_empty(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
405	    rbtdrf_run_ev(dir,
406	        "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Booleans\"\n\
407	         buv_bool_enroll TEST_ENABLED \"Feature enabled\"",

409	    )

412	fn rbtdrf_ev_enum_valid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {

414	        "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Enums\"\n\
415	         buv_enum_enroll TEST_MODE \"Operating mode\" debug release test",
416	        &[
417	            RbtdrfSub::ok("first choice", "export TEST_MODE=\"debug\""),
418	            RbtdrfSub::ok("last choice", "export TEST_MODE=\"test\""),
419	        ],
420	    )
421	}
422	
423	fn rbtdrf_ev_enum_invalid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {

427	        &[
428	            RbtdrfSub::fatal("not a choice", "export TEST_MODE=\"production\""),
429	            RbtdrfSub::fatal("case mismatch", "export TEST_MODE=\"Debug\""),
430	        ],
431	    )
432	}
433

523	// --- Reference types ---
524	
525	const RBTDRF_VALID_DIGEST: &str =
526	    "sha256:abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789";
527	
528	fn rbtdrf_ev_odref_valid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
529	    let root = match std::env::current_dir() {
530	        Ok(r) => r,
531	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("cannot get cwd: {}", e)),
532	    };
533	    let buv = root.join("Tools/buk/buv_validation.sh");
534	    let enrollment = format!(
535	        "set -euo pipefail\nsource '{}'\nzbuv_kindle\nzbuv_reset_enrollment\n\
536	         buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"References\"\n\
537	         buv_odref_enroll TEST_IMAGE \"Container image\"",
538	        rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&buv),
539	    );
540	    let d = RBTDRF_VALID_DIGEST;
541	
542	    let subs: &[(&str, &str)] = &[
543	        ("standard registry", &format!("docker.io/library/alpine@{}", d)),
544	        ("multi-level repo", &format!("us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-proj/my-repo/tool@{}", d)),
545	        ("registry with port", &format!("registry.local:5000/myimage@{}", d)),
546	    ];
547	
548	    for (i, (label, image)) in subs.iter().enumerate() {
549	        let script = format!("{}\nexport TEST_IMAGE=\"{}\"\nbuv_vet \"TEST\"", enrollment, image);
550	        match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script, dir, &format!("sub-{}", i)) {
551	            Ok((0, _, _)) => {}
552	            Ok((code, _, _)) => {
553	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected ok, got exit {}", label, code));
554	            }
555	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: {}", label, e)),
556	        }
557	    }
558	    rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
559	}

791	fn rbtdrf_ev_multiscope(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
792	    let root = match std::env::current_dir() {
793	        Ok(r) => r,
794	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("cannot get cwd: {}", e)),
795	    };
796	    let buv = root.join("Tools/buk/buv_validation.sh");
797	
798	    // Two-scope enrollment: ALPHA and BETA
799	    let enrollment = format!(
800	        "set -euo pipefail\nsource '{}'\nzbuv_kindle\nzbuv_reset_enrollment\n\
801	         buv_regime_enroll \"ALPHA\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Alpha Vars\"\n\
802	         buv_bool_enroll TEST_ALPHA_FLAG \"Alpha flag\"\n\
803	         buv_regime_enroll \"BETA\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Beta Vars\"\n\
804	         buv_bool_enroll TEST_BETA_FLAG \"Beta flag\"\n\
805	         export TEST_ALPHA_FLAG=\"1\"\nexport TEST_BETA_FLAG=\"bad\"\n",
806	        rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&buv),
807	    );
808	
809	    // Sub 1: vet ALPHA passes (BETA is bad but not in scope)
810	    let script1 = format!("{}buv_vet \"ALPHA\"", enrollment);
811	    match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script1, dir, "sub-0") {
812	        Ok((code, _, _)) if code == 0 => {}
813	        Ok((code, _, _)) => {
814	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("alpha scope: expected ok, got exit {}", code));
815	        }
816	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("alpha scope: {}", e)),
817	    }
818	
819	    // Sub 2: vet BETA fails
820	    let script2 = format!("{}buv_vet \"BETA\"", enrollment);
821	    match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script2, dir, "sub-1") {
822	        Ok((code, _, _)) if code != 0 => {}
823	        Ok((_, _, _)) => {
824	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail("beta scope: expected failure, got exit 0".to_string());
825	        }
826	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("beta scope: {}", e)),
827	    }
828	
829	    rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
830	}

832	// ── Regime-validation cases ─────────────────────────────────
833	
834	// Env-var name consts — single source of truth for prefix-related env vars
835	// referenced across rv_rbrr negatives and rs_rbrr_nonempty_prefix smoke.
836	const RBTDRF_VAR_RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX: &str = "RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX";
837	const RBTDRF_VAR_RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX: &str = "RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX";
838	
839	// Expected RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT value — must match RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS.
840	const RBTDRF_VAL_HALLMARKS_ROOT: &str = "rbi_hm";
841	
842	// --- RBRR negative tests ---
843	
844	// Valid synthetic RBRR baseline — every enrolled field passes kindle+enforce.
845	// VESSEL_DIR/SECRETS_DIR point at /tmp (existence-checked by buv_dir_exists;
846	// the bad-dir cases below override to a nonexistent path). Each negative
847	// applies exactly one violating override.
848	const RBTDRF_RBRR_BASELINE: &str = "\
849	export RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX=\"rb-\"\n\
850	export RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=\"/tmp\"\n\
851	export RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE=\"/workspace\"\n\
852	export RBRR_DNS_SERVER=\"8.8.8.8\"\n\
853	export RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT=\"1200s\"\n\
854	export RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS=\"1\"\n\
855	export RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=\"/tmp\"\n\
856	export RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL=\"https://example.com/docs\"";
857	
858	fn rbtdrf_rv_rbrr_neg(dir: &Path, label: &str, override_: &str) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
859	    rbtdrf_run_probate(dir, RBTDRM_MODULE_RBRR, RBTDRM_PROBATE_RBRR,
860	        RBTDRF_RBRR_BASELINE, override_, false, label)
861	}

865	        RBTDRF_RBRR_BASELINE, "", true, "rbrr-baseline-valid")
866	}

1	// Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
2	//
3	// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
4	// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
5	// You may obtain a copy of the License at
6	//

9	// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
10	// distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
11	// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
12	// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
13	// limitations under the License.
14	//
15	// Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
16	//
17	// RBTDRM — colophon manifest verification for theurge
18	
19	// Colophon names are projected from the zipper registry into the generated
20	// RBTDGC_* consts (rbtdgc_consts.rs). This module consumes them for the
21	// per-fixture required-colophon manifest and the role→probe mapping. Colophon
22	// existence is now enforced by compilation (this map references the generated
23	// consts directly) plus the build-time diff gate (rbq regenerates and diffs
24	// the consts against the zipper); the former runtime drift check is retired.
25	use crate::rbtdgc_consts::*;
26	
27	// Credential roles are projected from rbcc_Constants.sh into the generated
28	// RBTDGC_ACCOUNT_* consts (rbtdgc_consts.rs) — consumed here and across the access
29	// probe surface. The former hand-written RBTDRM_ROLE_* mirror is retired.
30	
31	/// Map a credential role to its access-probe colophon. Returns None for
32	/// unknown roles. Replaces the former role-as-imprint scheme: each role now
33	/// names its own global tabtarget under the rbw-ac* family.
34	pub fn rbtdrm_credential_check_colophon(role: &str) -> Option<&'static str> {
35	    match role {
36	        RBTDGC_ACCOUNT_GOVERNOR => Some(RBTDGC_CHECK_GOVERNOR),
37	        RBTDGC_ACCOUNT_RETRIEVER => Some(RBTDGC_CHECK_RETRIEVER),
38	        RBTDGC_ACCOUNT_DIRECTOR => Some(RBTDGC_CHECK_DIRECTOR),
39	        RBTDGC_ACCOUNT_PAYOR => Some(RBTDGC_CHECK_PAYOR),
40	        _ => None,
41	    }
42	}
43	
44	// Fixture name consts — single definition per String Boundary Discipline.
45	// Crucible fixtures (charge/quench lifecycle)
46	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_TADMOR: &str = "tadmor";
47	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_MORIAH: &str = "moriah";
48	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_SRJCL: &str = "srjcl";
49	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PLUML: &str = "pluml";
50	// Bare fixtures (GCP credentials, no container runtime)
51	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_HALLMARK_LIFECYCLE: &str = "hallmark-lifecycle";
52	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_BATCH_VOUCH: &str = "batch-vouch";
53	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_ACCESS_PROBE: &str = "access-probe";
54	// Fast fixtures (no external dependencies)
55	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_ENROLLMENT_VALIDATION: &str = "enrollment-validation";
56	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_REGIME_VALIDATION: &str = "regime-validation";
57	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_REGIME_SMOKE: &str = "regime-smoke";
58	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_HANDBOOK_RENDER: &str = "handbook-render";
59	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_DOCKERFILE_HYGIENE: &str = "dockerfile-hygiene";
60	// Pristine-lifecycle fixture (gate + SA/depot lifecycle cases)
61	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE: &str = "pristine-lifecycle";
62	// Gauntlet canonical-establish fixture (§2: canonical depot levy + governor
63	// mantle + retriever/director invest with per-case precondition probes)
64	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_CANONICAL_ESTABLISH: &str = "canonical-establish";
65	// Skirmish canonical-invest fixture — the no-levy variant of
66	// canonical-establish. Reuses the three investiture cases (governor mantle +
67	// retriever/director invest) against a depot the operator has already levied
68	// by hand; omits depot-levy so the skirmish suite creates no GCP project per
69	// run. Distinction from canonical-establish is precondition, not behavior.
70	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_CANONICAL_INVEST: &str = "canonical-invest";
71	// Gauntlet onboarding-sequence fixture (§3: handbook-walked vessel
72	// construction — inscribe reliquary, enshrine bases, kludge tadmor/ccyolo,
73	// plus one ordain-* case per director-mode handbook track, build-only)
74	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_ONBOARDING_SEQUENCE: &str = "onboarding-sequence";
75	// Self-contained tadmor build fixture (rbw-ts.TestSuite.tadmor): kludges tadmor sentry+bottle
76	// locally and commits each hallmark (so the subsequent tadmor crucible fixture
77	// charges against a clean nameplate). Reuses onboarding's kludge helper minus
78	// its reliquary-stamp witness probe — local kludge has no GCP/reliquary dep.
79	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_KLUDGE_TADMOR: &str = "kludge-tadmor";
80	// Dogfight cloud-build viability fixture — standing-depot sibling to
81	// canonical-invest, proving the cloud-build → summon → run path yields a
82	// runnable artifact with NO crucible charged (the orthogonal axis skirmish
83	// covers). Ordains conjure-mode busybox, summons it, runs a degenerate
84	// container-runtime command proving executability, then abjures.
85	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_DOGFIGHT: &str = "dogfight";
86	// Calibrant fixtures — synthetic deterministic-verdict fixtures driving the
87	// bash blackbox testbench. Internal framework-test plumbing.
88	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_CALIBRANT_VERDICTS: &str = "calibrant-verdicts";
89	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_CALIBRANT_FAIL_FAST: &str = "calibrant-fail-fast";
90	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_CALIBRANT_PROGRESSING: &str = "calibrant-progressing";
91	pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_CALIBRANT_SENTINEL: &str = "calibrant-sentinel";
92	
93	// Operation verbs and container roles are generated as RBTDGC_VERB_* and
94	// RBTDGC_CONTAINER_* (rbtdgc_consts.rs) from their canonical bash home in
95	// rbcc_Constants.sh; consumers source those directly. Compound operations like
96	// "kludge sentry" are composed at the call site from RBTDGC_VERB_KLUDGE and the
97	// relevant RBTDGC_CONTAINER_* constant.
98	
99	// Regime-validation contract surfaces — the regime module theurge sources and
100	// the public *_probate entry it calls to drive a staged regime file through
101	// kindle+enforce. Single definition per String Boundary Discipline.
102	pub const RBTDRM_MODULE_RBRR: &str = "rbrr_regime.sh";
103	pub const RBTDRM_PROBATE_RBRR: &str = "rbrr_probate";
104	pub const RBTDRM_MODULE_RBRD: &str = "rbrd_regime.sh";
105	pub const RBTDRM_PROBATE_RBRD: &str = "rbrd_probate";
106	pub const RBTDRM_MODULE_RBRV: &str = "rbrv_regime.sh";
107	pub const RBTDRM_PROBATE_RBRV: &str = "rbrv_probate";
108	pub const RBTDRM_MODULE_RBRN: &str = "rbrn_regime.sh";
109	pub const RBTDRM_PROBATE_RBRN: &str = "rbrn_probate";
110	
111	/// Per-fixture required colophons. Returns None for unknown fixtures.
112	pub fn rbtdrm_required_colophons(fixture: &str) -> Option<&'static [&'static str]> {
113	    match fixture {
114	        RBTDRM_FIXTURE_TADMOR
115	        | RBTDRM_FIXTURE_MORIAH
116	        | RBTDRM_FIXTURE_SRJCL
117	        | RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PLUML => Some(&[
118	            RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE,
119	            RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_QUENCH,
120	            RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_WRIT,
121	            RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_FIAT,
122	            RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_BARK,
123	            RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_ACTIVE,
124	        ]),
125	        RBTDRM_FIXTURE_HALLMARK_LIFECYCLE => Some(&[
126	            RBTDGC_ORDAIN_HALLMARK,
127	            RBTDGC_ABJURE_HALLMARK,
128	            RBTDGC_REKON_HALLMARK,
129	            RBTDGC_AUDIT_HALLMARKS,
130	        ]),
131	        RBTDRM_FIXTURE_BATCH_VOUCH => Some(&[
132	            RBTDGC_ORDAIN_HALLMARK,
133	            RBTDGC_ABJURE_HALLMARK,
134	            RBTDGC_JETTISON_HALLMARK_IMAGE,
135	            RBTDGC_VOUCH_HALLMARKS,
136	            RBTDGC_TALLY_HALLMARKS,
137	        ]),
138	        RBTDRM_FIXTURE_ACCESS_PROBE => Some(&[
139	            RBTDGC_CHECK_GOVERNOR,
140	            RBTDGC_CHECK_RETRIEVER,
141	            RBTDGC_CHECK_DIRECTOR,
142	            RBTDGC_CHECK_PAYOR,
143	        ]),
144	        RBTDRM_FIXTURE_ENROLLMENT_VALIDATION
145	        | RBTDRM_FIXTURE_REGIME_VALIDATION
146	        | RBTDRM_FIXTURE_REGIME_SMOKE
147	        | RBTDRM_FIXTURE_CALIBRANT_VERDICTS
148	        | RBTDRM_FIXTURE_CALIBRANT_FAIL_FAST
149	        | RBTDRM_FIXTURE_CALIBRANT_PROGRESSING
150	        | RBTDRM_FIXTURE_CALIBRANT_SENTINEL => Some(&[]),
151	        RBTDRM_FIXTURE_DOCKERFILE_HYGIENE => Some(&[
152	            RBTDGC_HYGIENE_CHECK_DOCKERFILE,
153	            RBTDGC_HYGIENE_CHECK_VESSEL,
154	        ]),
155	        RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE => Some(&[
156	            RBTDGC_LEVY_DEPOT,
157	            RBTDGC_LIST_DEPOT,
158	            RBTDGC_UNMAKE_DEPOT,
159	            RBTDGC_MANTLE_GOVERNOR,
160	            RBTDGC_INVEST_RETRIEVER,
161	            RBTDGC_INVEST_DIRECTOR,
162	            RBTDGC_DIVEST_RETRIEVER,
163	            RBTDGC_DIVEST_DIRECTOR,
164	            RBTDGC_CHECK_RETRIEVER,
165	            RBTDGC_CHECK_DIRECTOR,
166	        ]),
167	        RBTDRM_FIXTURE_CANONICAL_ESTABLISH => Some(&[
168	            RBTDGC_LEVY_DEPOT,
169	            RBTDGC_LIST_DEPOT,
170	            RBTDGC_MANTLE_GOVERNOR,
171	            RBTDGC_INVEST_RETRIEVER,
172	            RBTDGC_INVEST_DIRECTOR,
173	            RBTDGC_CHECK_RETRIEVER,
174	            RBTDGC_CHECK_DIRECTOR,
175	        ]),
176	        // canonical-invest reuses canonical-establish's investiture cases sans
177	        // levy — same colophons minus LEVY_DEPOT (depot is operator-provided).
178	        RBTDRM_FIXTURE_CANONICAL_INVEST => Some(&[
179	            RBTDGC_LIST_DEPOT,
180	            RBTDGC_MANTLE_GOVERNOR,
181	            RBTDGC_INVEST_RETRIEVER,
182	            RBTDGC_INVEST_DIRECTOR,
183	            RBTDGC_CHECK_RETRIEVER,
184	            RBTDGC_CHECK_DIRECTOR,
185	        ]),
186	        // kludge-tadmor builds both vessels locally; only the two kludge
187	        // colophons are exercised (no charge/credential colophons here).
188	        RBTDRM_FIXTURE_KLUDGE_TADMOR => Some(&[
189	            RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_SENTRY,
190	            RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE,
191	        ]),
192	        // dogfight ordains/summons/abjures a single conjure-mode hallmark; the
193	        // bare container-runtime run is shelled directly, not via a colophon.
194	        RBTDRM_FIXTURE_DOGFIGHT => Some(&[
195	            RBTDGC_ORDAIN_HALLMARK,
196	            RBTDGC_SUMMON_HALLMARK,
197	            RBTDGC_ABJURE_HALLMARK,
198	        ]),
199	        RBTDRM_FIXTURE_ONBOARDING_SEQUENCE => Some(&[
200	            RBTDGC_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY,
201	            RBTDGC_YOKE_RELIQUARY,
202	            RBTDGC_ENSHRINE_VESSEL,
203	            RBTDGC_ORDAIN_HALLMARK,
204	            RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_SENTRY,
205	            RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE,
206	            RBTDGC_WREST_HALLMARK_IMAGE,
207	            RBTDGC_SUMMON_HALLMARK,
208	            RBTDGC_PLUMB_FULL,
209	            RBTDGC_PLUMB_COMPACT,
210	            RBTDGC_REKON_HALLMARK,
211	            RBTDGC_JETTISON_HALLMARK_IMAGE,
212	            RBTDGC_ABJURE_HALLMARK,
213	        ]),
214	        RBTDRM_FIXTURE_HANDBOOK_RENDER => Some(&[
215	            RBTDGC_ONBOARD_START_HERE,
216	            RBTDGC_ONBOARD_CRASH_COURSE,
217	            RBTDGC_ONBOARD_CRED_RETRIEVER,
218	            RBTDGC_ONBOARD_CRED_DIRECTOR,
219	            RBTDGC_ONBOARD_FIRST_CRUCIBLE,
220	            RBTDGC_ONBOARD_DIR_FIRST_BUILD,
221	            RBTDGC_ONBOARD_PAYOR_HB,
222	            RBTDGC_ONBOARD_GOVERNOR_HB,

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.

# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle GCP Constants - Implementation (no printf required)

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
# (Module state remains ZRBGC_* per BCG; external constants use RBGC_*)
test -z "${ZRBGC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBGC_SOURCED=1

# Tinder constants (pure string literals, no variable expansion — available at source time)
# Depot project ID infix between RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX and RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER, consumed
# by rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh's RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID derivation.
RBGC_depot_project_infix="d-"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbgc_*)

zrbgc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc already kindled"

  # Global Resource Naming (Google Cloud global namespace)
  # These resources compete in globally-unique namespaces across all of GCP
  # Pattern: {prefix}-{type}-{name}-{timestamp} where timestamp is YYMMDDHHMMSS
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX="rbwg"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR="p"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT="d"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET="b"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT="+%y%m%d%H%M%S"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN=12
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX="[0-9]{${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN}}"

  # Global resource validation patterns
  # Payor:  rbwg-p-YYMMDDHHMMSS  (timestamp survives — payor is installation-scoped, not depot-scoped)
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PAYOR_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"

  # Basic Configuration
  readonly RBGC_ADMIN_ROLE="rbw-admin"

content.text:
I have complete ground truth. Design decisions:

- **8 bash probates**, all uniform copies of `rbrr_probate` (source→kindle→enforce). `rbrp`'s RBGC dependency is satisfied by a harness *prelude* (mirroring how the smoke test `rbtdrf_rs_rbrr_nonempty_prefix` already sources+kindles rbgc in the Rust harness), keeping all 8 bash functions identical — true to the "uniform mechanical replication" character. RBGC kindle is self-contained (pure literal constants).
- **Rust harness** generalizes to a `tools_subdir` (Tools/rbk vs Tools/buk) + optional prereqs, with a thin wrapper preserving the 4 existing RBK call sites unchanged.

Let me write the bash probates first.

    [[ "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID}" =~ \.apps\.googleusercontent\.com$ ]] \
      || buc_die "RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID must end with .apps.googleusercontent.com"
  fi
}

if test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID}"; then
    [[ "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID}" =~ \.apps\.googleusercontent\.com$ ]] \
      || buc_die "RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID must end with .apps.googleusercontent.com"
  fi
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (rbrp_*)

# Source an arbitrary RBRP regime file and run the full kindle->enforce
# chain against it, failing on first fault. Test-facing contract surface:
# theurge drives synthetic-malformed regime files through this without
# reaching module internals. Prerequisites: buv kindled, and RBGC kindled
# (zrbrp_enforce reaches RBGC_GLOBAL_PAYOR_REGEX for the payor-project check).
rbrp_probate() {
  local -r z_file="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrp_probate: regime file argument required"
  source "${z_file}"  || buc_die "rbrp_probate: cannot source ${z_file}"
  zrbrp_kindle
  zrbrp_enforce
}

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Payor Regime - Validator Module

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBRP_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrp multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBRP_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbrp_*)

zrbrp_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBRP_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrp already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all RBRP variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll RBRP

  buv_group_enroll "Payor Project Identity"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID  1  128  "GCP project hosting OAuth client"

  buv_group_enroll "Billing Configuration"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID  0  20  "Billing account for depot projects"

  buv_group_enroll "OAuth Configuration"
  # min=0 deliberate — not every operator has a Payor identity (Retriever-only
  # operators authenticate via JWT SA, never via Payor OAuth). Required-at-use
  # is enforced by test -n in rbgp_Payor.sh consumers. Do not tighten to min=1.
  buv_string_enroll  RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID  0  256  "OAuth 2.0 client identifier"

  buv_group_enroll "Operator Identity"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRP_OPERATOR_EMAIL  0  256  "Operator Google account email for console access"

  # Guard against unexpected RBRP_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel RBRP RBRP_

  # Lock all enrolled RBRP_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock RBRP

  readonly ZRBRP_KINDLED=1
}

zrbrp_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBRP_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrp not kindled - call zrbrp_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all RBRP enrollment validations and custom format checks
zrbrp_enforce() {
  zrbrp_sentinel

  buv_vet RBRP

  # Custom format checks beyond buv_ type system
  zrbgc_sentinel
  [[ "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID}" =~ ${RBGC_GLOBAL_PAYOR_REGEX} ]] \
    || buc_die "RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID does not match payor project pattern"

  if test -n "${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}"; then
    [[ "${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}" =~ ^[A-Z0-9]{6}-[A-Z0-9]{6}-[A-Z0-9]{6}$ ]] \
      || buc_die "RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID must be XXXXXX-XXXXXX-XXXXXX format"
  fi

  if test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID}"; then
    [[ "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID}" =~ \.apps\.googleusercontent\.com$ ]] \
      || buc_die "RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID must end with .apps.googleusercontent.com"
  fi
}

# Recipe Bottle OAuth Regime - Validator Module

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBRO_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbro multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBRO_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbro_*)

zrbro_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBRO_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbro already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all RBRO variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering
  buv_regime_enroll RBRO

  buv_group_enroll "OAuth Credentials"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET  1  512  "OAuth client secret"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN  1  512  "OAuth refresh token"

  # Guard against unexpected RBRO_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel RBRO RBRO_

  # Lock all enrolled RBRO_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock RBRO

  readonly ZRBRO_KINDLED=1
}

zrbro_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBRO_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbro not kindled - call zrbro_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all RBRO enrollment validations
zrbro_enforce() {
  zrbro_sentinel
  buv_vet RBRO
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (rbro_*)

# Load RBRO from RBDC_PAYOR_RBRO_FILE (kindle constant from RBRR_SECRETS_DIR)
# Requires: RBRR kindled+enforced+locked (RBDC_PAYOR_RBRO_FILE is a lock-step constant)
rbro_load() {
  local z_rbro_file="${RBDC_PAYOR_RBRO_FILE}"

  test -f "${z_rbro_file}" || buc_die "RBRO credentials missing (${z_rbro_file}) - run rbgp_payor_install"
  test -r "${z_rbro_file}" || buc_die "RBRO file not readable - check permissions"

  # Source and validate
  source "${z_rbro_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRO credentials"
  zrbro_kindle
  zrbro_enforce
}

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#

# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Authentication Regime - Validator Module

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBRA_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbra multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBRA_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbra_*)

zrbra_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBRA_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbra already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all RBRA variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll RBRA

  buv_group_enroll "Role Identity"
  buv_string_enroll   RBRA_ROLE                1    20  "Service account role (rbnae_governor|rbnae_retriever|rbnae_director)"

  buv_group_enroll "Service Account Credentials"
  buv_string_enroll   RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL        1   256  "Service account email address"
  buv_secret_enroll   RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY         1  4096  "PEM-encoded private key material"
  buv_string_enroll   RBRA_PROJECT_ID          1    64  "GCP project owning the service account"
  buv_decimal_enroll  RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC  300  3600  "OAuth token lifetime in seconds"

  # Guard against unexpected RBRA_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel RBRA RBRA_

  # Lock all enrolled RBRA_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock RBRA

  readonly ZRBRA_KINDLED=1
}

zrbra_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBRA_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbra not kindled - call zrbra_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all RBRA enrollment validations plus custom format checks
zrbra_enforce() {
  zrbra_sentinel

  buv_vet RBRA

  # RBRA_ROLE must be a valid role name (minted rbnae_ enum sprue)
  case "${RBRA_ROLE}" in
    rbnae_governor|rbnae_retriever|rbnae_director) ;;
    *) buc_die "RBRA_ROLE must be rbnae_governor, rbnae_retriever, or rbnae_director — got: '${RBRA_ROLE}'" ;;
  esac

  # Client email must match service account pattern
  [[ "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL}" =~ \.iam\.gserviceaccount\.com$ ]] \
    || buc_die "RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL does not match service account pattern (*.iam.gserviceaccount.com): '${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL}'"

  # Private key must contain PEM key material
  [[ "${RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY}" =~ BEGIN ]] \
    || buc_die "RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY does not contain PEM key material"
}

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at

# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURC Regime - Bash Utility Regime Configuration Module

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURC_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zburc_*)

zburc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burc already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.
  # Exception: BURC_BUK_DIR is derived from BURC_TOOLS_DIR at kindle time (before vet).
  readonly BURC_BUK_DIR="${BURC_TOOLS_DIR:-}/buk"

  # Enroll all BURC variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll BURC

  buv_group_enroll "Station Reference"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_STATION_FILE          1  512  "Path to developer's BURS station file"

  buv_group_enroll "Tabtarget Infrastructure"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_TABTARGET_DIR         1  128  "Project dir containing launcher scripts"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_TABTARGET_DELIMITER   1    1  "Token separator in tabtarget filenames"

  buv_group_enroll "Project Structure"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_TOOLS_DIR             1  128  "Project dir containing tool scripts"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_PROJECT_ROOT          1  512  "Repo root, expressed relative to burc.env"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_MANAGED_KITS          1  512  "Comma-separated kit list for vvx"

  buv_group_enroll "Build Output"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_TEMP_ROOT_DIR         1  512  "Parent dir for per-dispatch scratch subdirs (temp-<stamp>)"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR       1  512  "Parent dir containing 'current/', cleared and recreated each dispatch"

  buv_group_enroll "Logging"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURC_LOG_LAST              1   64  "Filename stem for last-run log"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURC_LOG_EXT               1   16  "Log file extension (without dot)"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_BUK_DIR               1  256  "Derived: BUK directory"

  # Guard against unexpected BURC_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURC BURC_

  # Export and lock all enrolled BURC_ variables — committed config,
  # needed by coordinator child processes across exec boundary
  buv_export_and_lock BURC

  readonly ZBURC_KINDLED=1
}

zburc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burc not kindled - call zburc_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURC enrollment validations
zburc_enforce() {
  zburc_sentinel

  buv_vet BURC
}

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.

#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURN Regime - Bash Utility Regime Node Module
#
# BURN is a multi-instance regime — one file per node investiture.
# Each instance lives at rbmm_moorings/rbmn_nodes/<investiture>/burn.env and is
# project-global (git-tracked, shared across station users).

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURN_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burn multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURN_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zburn_*)

zburn_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURN_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burn already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  buv_regime_enroll BURN

  buv_group_enroll "Node Identity"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_HOST      1  253  "IP address or hostname of the remote node"
  buv_enum_enroll    BURN_PLATFORM  "OS family of the node (bunne_* identifier)"  bunne_linux bunne_mac bunne_windows

  # Guard against unexpected BURN_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURN BURN_

  # Lock all enrolled BURN_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURN

  readonly ZBURN_KINDLED=1
}

zburn_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURN_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burn not kindled - call zburn_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURN enrollment validations
zburn_enforce() {
  zburn_sentinel

  buv_vet BURN
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (burn_*)

# List available investiture names as space-separated tokens.
# Emits one <investiture>.${BUF_EXT_ALIAS} observation fact per profile
# (empty content; presence is the fact).
# Returns non-zero if no profiles are present.
# Prerequisite: BURD kindled (BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burn_list_capture() {
  zburd_sentinel

  local -r z_nodes_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/${BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir}"
  test -d "${z_nodes_dir}" || return 1

  local z_result=""
  local z_files=("${z_nodes_dir}"/*/burn.env)
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
    local z_investiture="${z_dir##*/}"
    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_investiture}" "${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}" ""
    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_investiture}"
  done
  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
  echo "${z_result}"
}

# Friendly-error die: emit "no folio supplied" message followed by available
# investitures (or a "no profiles" hint when the directory is empty).
# Prerequisite: BURD kindled (BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burn_die_no_folio() {
  zburd_sentinel
  local z_aliases=""
  if z_aliases=$(burn_list_capture 2>/dev/null); then
    buc_warn "BURN investiture required as first argument."
    buc_step "Available investitures under ${BUBC_moorings_dir}/${BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir}/:"
    local z_v=""
    for z_v in ${z_aliases}; do
      buc_bare "        ${z_v}"
    done
  else
    buc_warn "BURN investiture required as first argument."
    buc_step "No profiles found under ${BUBC_moorings_dir}/${BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir}/."
    buc_bare "        Author one at ${BUBC_moorings_dir}/${BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir}/<investiture>/burn.env"
  fi
  buc_die "No BURN investiture supplied."
}

#
# BURP Regime - Bash Utility Regime Privileged Module
#
# BURP is a multi-instance regime — one file per privileged investiture.
# Each instance lives at rbmm_moorings/rbmu_users/<BURS_USER>/<investiture>/burp.env
# and is per-station-user (operator-authored, not git-tracked).

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURP_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burp multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURP_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zburp_*)

zburp_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURP_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burp already kindled"

  buv_regime_enroll BURP

  buv_group_enroll "Privileged Credentials"
  buv_string_enroll  BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER      1   64  "Remote admin OS user this investiture authenticates as"
  buv_string_enroll  BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE  1  512  "Path to the operator-managed SSH private key authorized as the privileged user"

  buv_group_enroll "Workload Credentials"
  buv_string_enroll  BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE    1  512  "Path to the operator-managed SSH private key the workload account uses for inbound and outbound auth"

  # Guard against unexpected BURP_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURP BURP_

  # Lock all enrolled BURP_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURP

  readonly ZBURP_KINDLED=1
}

zburp_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURP_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burp not kindled - call zburp_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURP enrollment validations
zburp_enforce() {
  zburp_sentinel

  buv_vet BURP
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (burp_*)

# List available investiture names as space-separated tokens for the current
# BURS_USER. Emits one <investiture>.${BUF_EXT_ALIAS} observation fact per
# profile (empty content; presence is the fact).
# Returns non-zero if no profiles are present.
# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (BURS_USER), BURD kindled (BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burp_list_capture() {
  zburs_sentinel

  local -r z_user_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/${BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir}/${BURS_USER}"
  test -d "${z_user_dir}" || return 1

  local z_result=""
  local z_files=("${z_user_dir}"/*/burp.env)
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
    local z_investiture="${z_dir##*/}"
    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_investiture}" "${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}" ""
    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_investiture}"
  done
  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
  echo "${z_result}"
}

# Emit the available investitures listing (or "no profiles" hint when empty),
# followed by the BURN-side roster tabtarget advisory. Shared body for the
# no-folio and unknown-folio friendly-error paths.
# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (BURS_USER), BURD kindled (BURD_CONFIG_DIR);
# buym_yelp.sh and buh_handbook.sh sourced; buwz_zipper.sh kindled (for
# BUWZ_RN_LIST and the yawp/buh_line path).
burp_emit_available_investitures() {
  zburs_sentinel
  local z_aliases=""
  if z_aliases=$(burp_list_capture 2>/dev/null); then
    buc_step "Available investitures under ${BUBC_moorings_dir}/${BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir}/${BURS_USER}/:"
    local z_v=""
    for z_v in ${z_aliases}; do
      buc_bare "        ${z_v}"
    done
  else
    buc_step "No profiles found under ${BUBC_moorings_dir}/${BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir}/${BURS_USER}/."
    buc_bare "        Author one at ${BUBC_moorings_dir}/${BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir}/${BURS_USER}/<investiture>/burp.env"
  fi
  buyy_tt_yawp "${BUWZ_RN_LIST}";  local -r z_rnl="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line "Investiture names come from BURN — run ${z_rnl} for the registered list."
}

# Friendly-error die: no folio was supplied as first argument.
burp_die_no_folio() {
  buc_warn "BURP investiture required as first argument."
  burp_emit_available_investitures
  buc_die "No BURP investiture supplied."
}

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zburs_*)

zburs_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURS_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burs already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all BURS variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll BURS

  buv_group_enroll "Developer Identity"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURS_USER     1   32  "Local developer username (routes to ${BUBC_moorings_dir}/${BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir}/ profiles)"
  buv_string_enroll  BURS_TINCTURE 1    3  "Per-station tincture composed by test fixtures into cloud/runtime prefixes and family stems for parallel-run disjointness on a shared payor manor (lowercase alphanumeric, leading letter, no hyphen)"

  buv_group_enroll "Developer Logging"
  buv_string_enroll  BURS_LOG_DIR  1  512  "Directory for BUK operation logs"

  # Guard against unexpected BURS_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURS BURS_

  # Lock all enrolled BURS_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURS

  readonly ZBURS_KINDLED=1
}

zburs_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURS_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burs not kindled - call zburs_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURS enrollment validations
zburs_enforce() {
  zburs_sentinel

  buv_vet BURS

  [[ "${BURS_TINCTURE}" =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9]{0,2}$ ]] \
    || buc_die "Invalid BURS_TINCTURE format: ${BURS_TINCTURE} (expected 1-3 chars, lowercase alphanumeric starting with letter; no hyphens)"
}

=== secret/string length+charset handling (lines 340-375) ===
  local z_p2="${z_buv_p2_roll[$z_idx]}"

  # Gating check — if gated and gate doesn't match, skip (pass)
  if test -n "${z_gate_var}"; then
    local z_gate_actual="${!z_gate_var:-}"
    if test "${z_gate_actual}" != "${z_gate_val}"; then
      echo "${BUV_check_gated}"
      return 0
    fi
  fi

  # Unset detection — distinguish "not set" from "set but empty"
  if test -z "${!z_varname+x}"; then
    case "${z_type}" in
      string|secret|gname)
        if test "${z_p1}" = "0"; then return 0; fi ;;
      list_string|list_ipv4|list_gname|list_cidr|list_domain)
        return 0 ;;
    esac
    echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} is not set (missing from .env?)"

input.command:
cd /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster 2>/dev/null
BUV=Tools/buk/buv_validation.sh
run() { # name subdir module probate prereq baseline override expect(ok/fail)
  local name="$1" sub="$2" mod="$3" fn="$4" pre="$5" base="$6" ovr="$7" exp="$8"
  local ef; ef=$(mktemp)
  printf '%s\n%s\n' "$base" "$ovr" > "$ef"
  bash -c "set -euo pipefail; source '$BUV'; source '$sub/$mod'; zbuv_kindle; $pre $fn '$ef'" >/dev/null 2>&1
  local code=$?
  rm -f "$ef"
  if [ "$exp" = ok ]; then [ $code -eq 0 ] && echo "PASS $name (ok)" || echo "FAIL $name expected ok got $code"; \
  else [ $code -ne 0 ] && echo "PASS $name (rejects, $code)" || echo "FAIL $name expected reject got 0"; fi
}

The `(relpath, kindle_fn)` tuple can't express "set a var, then source (no kindle)". Let me generalize the prereq to a raw bash `prelude` string — more flexible. content.text:
All green. The work of ₢AUAAh is complete and verified.

## ₢AUAAh (regime-probate-homogeneity) — done

**What landed** (committed at `57a633a`):

- **8 bash probates**, each a uniform `source → kindle → enforce` seam mirroring `rbrr_probate`:
  - RBK: `rbrp_probate`, `rbrs_probate`, `rbro_probate`, `rbra_probate`
  - BUK: `burc_probate`, `burn_probate`, `burp_probate`, `burs_probate`
- **16 theurge fast-fixture cases** (one baseline-valid + one seeded-violation per regime), violations aimed at each regime's custom enforce check where one exists, else an unset required field:
  - `rbrp` → payor-project regex · `rbra` → PEM-BEGIN · `burn` → platform enum · `burs` → tincture regex
  - `rbrs`/`rbro`/`burc`/`burp` → unset required field
- **Harness generalization**: `rbtdrf_run_probate_in` adds a `tools_subdir` (Tools/rbk vs Tools/buk) and a raw-bash `prelude`; a thin `rbtdrf_run_probate` wrapper keeps the 4 reference `rv_` call sites untouched.
- **16 manifest consts** for the new module/probate pairs.

**Two per-regime wrinkles surfaced and handled** (both via the prelude, mirroring the existing `rbtdrf_rs_rbrr_nonempty_prefix` idiom rather than contaminating the bash probates):
- **rbrp** — enforce reaches `RBGC_GLOBAL_PAYOR_REGEX`; prelude stages `rbgc` kindled. (This was the docket's flagged rabbit hole — RBGC kindle turned out self-contained, no deeper chain.)
- **burs** — *unanticipated by the docket*: its `BURS_USER` enrollment help-string expands `${BUBC_moorings_dir}/${BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir}` under `set -u`, so the probate needs `bubc` sourced (with a synthetic `BURD_CONFIG_DIR`). Caught by the manual smoke test before commit.

**Verification** (Done criteria, all met):
- `rbw-tt` unit tests: **100 passed, 0 failed**
- `rbw-ts fast`: **129 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped** — regime-validation fixture grew 32 → 48 cases, all 16 new ones green.

Tree is clean. I have **not** wrapped the pace — ready to wrap ₢AUAAh when you confirm.

Violations aim at custom enforce checks where present (rbrp payor regex via RBGC, rbra PEM-BEGIN, burn platform enum, burs tincture regex) else an unset required field; credential regimes use synthetic non-secret baselines. Generalized rbtdrf_run_probate into rbtdrf_run_probate_in (tools_subdir for Tools/rbk vs Tools/buk + raw-bash prelude for cross-module prereqs), keeping a thin wrapper so the 4 reference rv_ call sites stay untouched. Two per-regime prereqs handled via prelude rather than contaminating the bash probates: rbrp stages rbgc kindled (docket-flagged; RBGC kindle proved self-contained), and burs stages bubc — an unanticipated wrinkle where BURS_USER's enrollment help-string expands BUBC_* under set -u, caught by manual smoke test before commit. Verified: rbw-tt 100 passed; rbw-ts fast 129 passed 0 failed (regime-validation fixture 32→48 cases). Code committed 57a633a."}

The wrap was a clean state transition — the code was already committed at `57a633a`, so no new staged changes (as expected; the work was notched mid-execution to clear the suite's clean-tree gate).

**Heat ₣AU now has one remaining pace:** ₢AUAA7 (run-all-onboarding-tracks) — the end-to-end onboarding walk, which as we discussed in the groom is naturally the heat's final acceptance gate. It needs live GCP credentials + container runtime, so it's a heavier, in-person session.

When you're ready for it: `/clear` then `mount AU`. Nice work today.

18	
19	// Colophon names are projected from the zipper registry into the generated
20	// RBTDGC_* consts (rbtdgc_consts.rs). This module consumes them for the
21	// per-fixture required-colophon manifest and the role→probe mapping. Colophon
22	// existence is now enforced by compilation (this map references the generated
23	// consts directly) plus the build-time diff gate (rbq regenerates and diffs
24	// the consts against the zipper); the former runtime drift check is retired.

Interactive design conversation requiring operator judgment — do NOT
implement. A strategic reorientation that should reshape this heat's tactical
paces before they are cut; its product is an agreed direction, not code.

## Trigger

Surfaced live while first-running the ₣BM skirmish suite. The project's active
Payor moved from a **consumer** Google account (`bhyslop@gmail.com`, no org) to
an **org-affiliated** Workspace identity (`bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org`, org
`247899326218`). The operator's read: this is a major use-style change, not a
one-off config tweak, and the project must be reoriented to treat
org-affiliated accounts as a first-class mode.

## What we proved this session (the durable reminder)

Org-affiliated identities inherit secure-by-default posture the prior consumer
account never had — two distinct behaviors, two distinct layers:

- **Reauth cadence.** The org's session/reauth policy refuses the payor OAuth
  refresh token after a short window (a ~22h-old token returned `invalid_rapt`;
  `rbw-gPI` re-auth cleared it). `rbw-gPR` is stale here — its text assumes the
  consumer "6-month idle expiry" model, which no longer describes reality.
- **Key-creation block.** `iam.disableServiceAccountKeyCreation` is `enforced`
  by default on the standing depot, so governor/retriever/director SA-key
  creation will be refused — the wall the mantle would hit next.
- The payor **lacks** `orgpolicy.policy.set` but **holds**
  `resourcemanager.organizations.setIamPolicy` (so it could self-grant
  policyAdmin). The standing depot is ACTIVE under the org; no levy needed.

## The memo this fold pulls in

`Memos/memo-20260427-google-native-human-auth.md` — a 2026-04-27 design
exploration proposing the project move off static RBRA service-account keyfiles
(Governor / Director / Retriever) toward formal Google identities via
**REST-only browser OAuth + SA impersonation** (Approach C; gcloud-SDK Approach
B dismissed for dependency surface; keyfile Approach A retained as coexisting).
Mechanism: user authenticates once in a browser, refresh token cached locally;
per operation, mint a user access token then call `generateAccessToken` on
`iamcredentials.googleapis.com` to obtain a short-lived SA access token. The
SA principal is preserved — RBSCIG contracts, RBGI machinery, role taxonomy,
audit identity all unchanged. Distribution dissolves (access becomes an IAM
binding); revocation becomes a single binding deletion; audit logs gain
`delegationInfo`. Read it in full before reshaping downstream paces — its
Mechanism Detail, Storage shape, IAM binding model, Coexistence, Threat Model,
and Open Questions sections are the design substrate and should NOT be
re-paraphrased here.

## How the org finding reframes the memo (the key turn)

The memo framed keyfile→impersonation as an **adoption/convenience** play — its
two named frictions are distribution logistics and revocation hygiene, and its
"Trigger to Revisit" is demand-driven (first external-user request, or first
time keyfile distribution *materially* blocks adoption). The org-policy finding
is a **third trigger the memo never anticipated**: on an org-affiliated depot,
`iam.disableServiceAccountKeyCreation` is enforced *by default*, so the RBRA
keyfile pattern is not "theoretically frictionful" — it is **structurally
unavailable** unless the operator deliberately weakens org policy. That flips
the posture: impersonation moves from future nice-to-have toward the
**org-native path**, while keyfile mode becomes the thing requiring a policy
override to keep alive.

## Issues to work through (none resolved — captured for a fresh head)

1. **Is impersonation load-bearing for the org mode, or still optional?** The
   memo's coexistence section keeps keyfiles for headless paths (Cloud Build,
   automated fixtures, Ifrit-style scenarios). Those also run inside the
   depot/org. Open: does the key-creation block hit them too, or do they have a
   different escape (workload identity, build-time SA)? The answer decides
   whether impersonation is "the human path" or "the only path."

2. **The memo predates the reauth-cadence finding and collides with it.**
   Approach C is literally "replicate the Payor OAuth pattern" and leans *harder*
   on OAuth refresh tokens — yet this session proved the org's session policy
   *refuses* aged refresh tokens (`invalid_rapt` at ~22h). The very primitive the
   memo calls "already proven" is the one the org now constrains. The
   impersonation flow inherits the same reauth cadence; the memo's "refresh token
   cached locally, mint on demand" story needs that caveat.

3. **`rbw-gPR` (PayorRefresh) is stale.** Its "6-month idle expiry" text
   describes the consumer model, not the org's short reauth window. Whatever
   direction we land, this surface needs rewriting — and it interacts with #2,
   since impersonation onboarding would carry the same reauth assumption forward
   if copied verbatim.

4. **Org-policy override ownership.** The payor holds
   `resourcemanager.organizations.setIamPolicy` (could self-grant policyAdmin)
   but lacks `orgpolicy.policy.set`. If keyfile mode is to survive on org
   depots, *someone* must own relaxing `disableServiceAccountKeyCreation` — and
   where that override is surfaced, owned, and documented (establish / levy /
   onboarding) is part of the org-mode design, not a side note.

5. **Memo's own Open Questions remain open and feed downstream paces:** storage
   location (regime-tree vs `$HOME`), OAuth client scope (project-wide vs
   per-Manor), onboarding handbook track shape (parallel RBHO* vs unified
   branching), probe coexistence / mode dispatch (RBSAJ, RBSAO, RBSAV), and mode
   field naming/placement. These live in the memo; this pace decides which
   become tactical paces in this heat.

## Goal

Decide, honestly and with the operator, how org-affiliated accounts become a
supported use style: where reauth cadence and the key-creation override are
surfaced, owned, and documented across establish / levy / onboarding; whether
the keyfile→impersonation move (per the 2026-04-27 memo) is now load-bearing for
the org mode rather than a future convenience; and whether the tactical
org-policy paces already slated in this heat are the right shape or need
recasting in light of the above. Output: an agreed design direction written back
into the paddock, with the downstream paces reshaped to match.

## Constraint

Produces understanding and a reshaped plan, not code. Implementation belongs to
the downstream paces, governed by this pace's conclusions.",

Intricate but mechanical — a behavior-preserving move/rename/resite sweep, plus one operator-approved scope addition: deletion of two vestigial pre-gateway clis (see OPEN ITEM — now RESOLVED). No new logic. The map below was derived under Opus 4.7, then critically re-reviewed and operator-approved under Opus 4.8; two structural changes resulted (rbuj merged into rbuh; transient predicate homed in rbgo). This revised map is the authoritative spec — do NOT re-derive or re-split. Detail exceeds normal docket discipline by explicit operator allowance because this pace executes imminently. Re-verify the as-discovered site lists with the grep recipes at mount (file paths are durable; line numbers are not — none are cited here).

## Goal
Dissolve `rbgu_Utility.sh` (the "Google Utility" grab-bag) into focused modules whose prefix matches what the code actually is, dropping the now-redundant infix. `rbgu` disappears entirely (`grep 'rbgu_\|ZRBGU_' Tools/rbk` returns nothing).

## Revised frozen map (3 new modules; rbgi + rbgo absorb the rest; deletions below)

rbuh — NEW, Utility/HTTP + the JSON/string + temp-file machinery it produces and consumes (11 fns). Owns the shared temp-file constants. This is the rbuh+rbuj MERGE: the prior map split them, but they are mutually dependent (rbuh's require-ok reads JSON via the json-field capture; 3 of the 4 JSON fns read rbuh's temp-file constants) and always co-sourced/co-kindled — splitting was non-load-bearing. One module:
- rbgu_http_request            -> rbuh_request
- rbgu_http_json               -> rbuh_json
- rbgu_http_require_ok         -> rbuh_require_ok
- rbgu_http_code_capture       -> rbuh_code_capture
- rbgu_poll_until_ok           -> rbuh_poll_until_ok
- rbgu_poll_until_gone         -> rbuh_poll_until_gone
- rbgu_json_valid_predicate    -> rbuh_json_valid_predicate
- rbgu_json_field_capture      -> rbuh_json_field_capture
- rbgu_jq_file_to_file_ok      -> rbuh_jq_file_to_file_ok
- rbgu_urlencode_capture       -> rbuh_urlencode_capture
- (the transient predicate does NOT go here — see rbgo below)

Note: the original `rbgu_http_json`/`rbgu_http_request` define BOTH the retry-looping JSON helper and the BCG-conformant single-shot primitive — both move to rbuh as named.

rbge — NEW, GCP REST / LRO over rbuh (4 fns):
- rbgu_http_json_lro_ok        -> rbge_lro_ok
- rbgu_newly_created_delay     -> rbge_newly_created_delay
- rbgu_api_enable              -> rbge_api_enable
- rbgu_error_message_capture   -> rbge_error_message_capture

rba — NEW, Auth (mode-neutral; depends on rbgo, touches NO temp-file machinery — verified) (5 fns):
- rbgu_get_governor_token_capture -> rba_get_governor_token_capture
- rbgu_authenticate_role_capture  -> rba_authenticate_role_capture
- rbgu_rbra_load                  -> rba_rbra_load
- rbgu_rbro_load                  -> rba_rbro_load
- rbgu_extract_json_to_rbra       -> rba_extract_json_to_rbra

rbgi — EXISTING (IAM), gains 3 fns:
- rbgu_jq_add_member_to_role_capture -> rbgi_jq_add_member_to_role_capture
- rbgu_provision_service_agent       -> rbgi_provision_service_agent
- rbgu_sa_email_capture              -> rbgi_sa_email_capture

rbgo — EXISTING (OAuth), gains 1 STATELESS fn (the transient-curl-exit predicate):
- rbgu_curl_status_is_transient_predicate -> rbgo_curl_status_is_transient_predicate
  Rationale (operator-approved): this is the one stateless, sentinel-free function, consumed by BOTH rbgo's own curl-retry path AND rbuh's retry loop. rbgo is the lowest module that performs curl and is a kindle-dependency of rbuh, so it is the lowest common ancestor that can hold a function with the dependency arrow pointing only downward. It sits beside rbgo's existing stateless cross-module helpers (the rbgo_base64_* family, explicitly documented "safe to call from any module regardless of kindle order"). rbgc was rejected: it is constants-only, no home for methods.

DELETE (operator-approved — do NOT relocate):
- rbgu_write_vanilla_json — dead code, no callers anywhere.
- ZRBGU_EMPTY_JSON — dead code, written in kindle, never read (every other module owns its own).
- rbgb_cli.sh — vestigial pre-gateway cli (see OPEN ITEM RESOLVED). Delete the whole file.
- rbga_cli.sh — vestigial pre-gateway cli (see OPEN ITEM RESOLVED). Delete the whole file.

## Shared temp-file machinery — rbuh owns it
The infix protocol: rbuh_json writes `${PREFIX}${infix}${POSTFIX}`; the capture, require-ok, and the moved rbgi/rbge functions read those files back by infix. rbuh is the owner:
- ZRBGU_PREFIX -> ZRBUH_PREFIX; ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON -> ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON; ZRBGU_POSTFIX_CODE -> ZRBUH_POSTFIX_CODE (defined in zrbuh_kindle).
- rbge and the moved rbgi functions reference ZRBUH_* -> their

input.command:
cd /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster 2>/dev/null

    | grep -A2 '"₣B' | grep -E '"₣B|silks|status' | tail -40
done 2>&1 | head -120

Now I have the decisive picture:

| Heat | BASE | OURS (upstream) | THEIRS (my commit) |
|------|------|------|------|
| ₣BR (rbk-16-gcp-hardening) | stabled, **0 paces** | **absent** (upstream retired) | stabled, 0 paces |
| ₣BS (rbk-10) | racing, 7 paces | racing, 7 paces (**identical to base**) | **absent** (my commit retired) |

So both heats should be removed: upstream retired the empty ₣BR; my commit retires ₣BS. Critically, **OURS's ₣BS is byte-identical to BASE** — upstream did no rbk-10 work, so retiring it discards nothing (and the staged `jjh_…rbk-10` archive captures those 7 paces). Untangled and verified. Here's what the conflict was and how I resolved it:

## The conflict

A single conflict in `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json` (JJ state) while replaying your commit `9953d674f` (which **retires ₣BS / rbk-10**). It was a heat-map divergence at the tail:

| Heat | Base | Upstream (origin) | Your commit |
|------|------|------|------|
| **₣BR** (rbk-16-gcp-hardening) | empty, stabled | **removed** (upstream retired it) | still present |
| **₣BS** (rbk-10) | racing, 7 paces | racing, 7 paces (**identical to base**) | **removed** (retired) |

Each side retired a *different* heat. The correct merge is **both gone**.

## Why it was safe (no work discarded)

- Upstream's **₣BS was byte-identical to base** — upstream did zero rbk-10 work, so your retirement loses nothing, and the 7 paces are captured in the staged `retired/jjh_…rbk-10` archive.
- **₣BR** was empty (0 paces) when upstream removed it — nothing to lose there either.

## How I resolved it

Rather than hand-merge the giant block (risking format corruption of machine-managed state), I confirmed JJ writes standard `json.dump(indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)` with no trailing newline (verified byte-identical round-trip), then regenerated the file as **"upstream state minus ₣BS"** (removed from both `jjgrn_heat_order` and `jjgrn_heats`). The diff vs upstream was **186 lines removed, 0 added** — a pure, minimal deletion, nothing reformatted.

Guardrails checked before staging: valid JSON, zero conflict markers, `heat_order ↔ heats` key parity, ₣AU intact, and **`jjx_validate` → "Gallops validation passed"**.

## Final state

- Rebase completed cleanly (all 9 commits replayed); **working tree clean**, **0 behind / 9 ahead** of origin.
- Your ₢AUAAh probate work survived intact and the rebased tree builds green.
- No forbidden git ops used — resolution was edit + `git add` + `git rebase --continue`.

You're ready to `git push` when you want. The `₣BS:R` retirement is now correctly reconciled with upstream's `₣BR` removal.
